stage_label: Audit — skeptical pushback on Stage-2 top-5

halt_condition_check.anomaly_detected: INTEGERS, Stage-2 rank #2, has a HARD AI BAN equivalent to NNTDM's. Discovered during Stage 3 web verification. Removed from candidates. This is exactly the failure-mode Stage 3 exists to catch; not a halt condition but a substantive revision of the ranking. Logged in discrepancy_log.json.

verified.verdict: PERMISSIVE WITH DISCLOSURE. Verified via https://www.ams.org/publications/journals/policies/UseofArtificialIntelligence (AMS adapted COPE Feb 2023 statement). AI tools cannot be co-authors but their use must be disclosed in the Materials-and-Methods-equivalent section; authors fully responsible for content. Manuscript's existing Computational and AI Disclosure section satisfies this verbatim.

Yes (AMS Council, Jan 2021). Verified via https://www.ams.org/publications/journals/policies/policies. This is a STRONG positive for pseudonymous publishing — referees do not see author identity during review.

CONFIRMED. Articles-in-press list contains MSC 11Y65/11Y16 papers (e.g. Baird-Kerr-Shparlinski 'Bohr sets generated by polynomials and Coppersmith's method...' mcom/4001; Rosenfeld 'The lonely runner conjecture holds for eight runners' mcom/4243; 'Correcting Lehman's theorem on Turing's method' mcom/3946). Verified via https://www.ams.org/cgi-bin/mstrack/accepted_papers/mcom.

MODERATE. No explicit 'null result for X-family' titles in current early-view, but corrections, verifications, and 'X-conjecture holds for N runners' style precision-bounded computational results are present. The manuscript's framing as a calibration / methodology contribution rather than 'we tried to find structure and failed' is essential — the Bailey-Borwein-Crandall 1997 Khinchin paper appeared here, giving a clear precedent for the topic.
LOW. Combined with double-anonymous review, ORCID-only submission is straightforward; no institutional letterhead required for AMS journal submissions.

rejection_risk_failure_modes: Editor may view 'null result' as insufficient novelty if the methodology-as-contribution framing is not foregrounded in the cover letter.
MComp referees often demand the search space be characterized in a way that bounds future positive claims; the manuscript's local-scope disclaimer is appropriate but must be emphasised.
Six fixed templates may be perceived as narrow; reviewer may request broader template enumeration before publication.
candidate_audits.style_scope_drift: Low. The manuscript already cites BBC97/FBA99/BBP97/BB01 (all MComp). Format adjustment is minimal (AMSLaTeX template required for acceptance, not submission).
candidate_audits.reviewer_pool_concern: Moderate. The PSLQ + Khinchin reviewer pool is small (Bailey, Borwein-school, Broadhurst, Crandall-school, Heim, etc.) and there is non-trivial overlap with Experimental Mathematics reviewers. Mitigation: double-anonymous review removes the 'eight prior Exp Math rejections' shadow on the cover sheet.
candidate_audits.audit_verdict: RETAIN as primary candidate. Strong scope match, AI-permissive with disclosure, double-anonymous review is a structural advantage for pseudonymous author.

verified.verdict: HARD BAN. Verified verbatim from https://math.colgate.edu/~integers/submit.html: 'Use of AI (artificial intelligence): Integers will not consider any article that makes use of artificial intelligence in producing mathematics, computer code, bibliographic information, or other content. This does not apply to the use of basic tools to improve the presentation, such as grammar and spelling.' Manuscript's existing Disclosure explicitly names Copilot (code generation) and Claude (synthesis) — both squarely within the ban.

rejection_risk_failure_modes: Submission would be returned without review under the AI-use clause.
candidate_audits.null_result_tolerance: n/a — venue excluded before this check
candidate_audits.audit_verdict: DROP — HARD-EXCLUDED. AI ban is functionally identical to the NNTDM ban already in the hard-exclusion list. Adding INTEGERS to the persistent hard-exclusion list going forward (see Stage 5 ledger update).

verified.verdict: CONFIRMED indirectly via INTEGERS Vol 25 #43 (Gladun-Chaudhary, 'Connecting Ramanujan's Theta Functions, the Rogers-Ramanujan Continued Fraction, and the Golden Ratio') showing the broader CF + Ramanujan-circle community is active; primary Ramanujan-Journal verification page blocked by Springer login wall. Springer's general scope statement for the journal (broad: areas influenced by Ramanujan including q-series, continued fractions, partitions, modular forms, number theory) is well-attested in the public record.

[UNVERIFIED — Springer page redirects to login]. Inferred from Springer Nature's published AI policy (disclosure required, no co-authorship), which applies across Springer journals. AEAL claim entry will note this is inferred, not directly verified.
LOW. Springer ORCID-only accepted. APC ~$3000 if author elects gold OA (subscription default has no APC).

rejection_risk_failure_modes: Khinchin-specific Khinchin-constant content is more typically MComp than Ramanujan Journal; the manuscript's PSLQ-as-search framing may be a stretch for RamJ's primary remit (q-series, modular, partitions).
Null result in a paper not centered on Ramanujan-circle objects could be flagged as out-of-scope by editor at desk.
candidate_audits.null_result_tolerance: [unverified, requires recent TOC scan]. Springer-side login wall prevented full audit. Inferred: moderate.
candidate_audits.style_scope_drift: Moderate. Khinchin/PSLQ is at the edge of RamJ's centroid (CF appears in RamJ but mostly through q-series / Rogers-Ramanujan lenses, not metric theory).
candidate_audits.reviewer_pool_concern: Different from Exp Math pool (less PSLQ-focused, more q-series-focused).
candidate_audits.audit_verdict: DOWNGRADE. Move to reserve. Scope is plausible but not centroid; verification incomplete due to paywall.

verified.verdict: [UNVERIFIED — Springer login wall]. Inferred: Springer Nature standard (permissive with disclosure).
[UNVERIFIED — login wall]. Broad number-theory remit per Springer description; computational papers accepted.
Founded under Andrew Granville editorship; check current editorial board for relevance — not verified in this audit.

rejection_risk_failure_modes: RNT skews toward analytic and arithmetic-geometric NT; pure metric-CF/PSLQ work less common in TOC.
candidate_audits.audit_verdict: DOWNGRADE. Move to reserve or drop; verification gaps too large to commit. Recommend re-audit if surfaced in a future relay.

candidate_audits.venue: Bulletin of the Australian Mathematical Society

verified.verdict: VERIFIED FAST. Cambridge/AustMS official statement: 'editorial decisions on acceptance or otherwise are taken quickly, normally within a month of receipt of the paper.' Source: https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/bulletin-of-the-australian-mathematical-society/information/about-this-journal and /information/author-instructions. This is the fastest decision time among all top-5 candidates.
VERIFIED. 'Papers longer than 12 pages are not usually accepted'. The 6-8 page Khinchin-PSLQ manuscript fits comfortably.

VERIFIED. 'Original research in all branches of mathematics' with explicit focus on 'new and interesting results in a clear and attractive way'. Short notes with computational content are routinely published.

PERMISSIVE WITH DISCLOSURE. Verified via https://www.cambridge.org/core/services/authors/publishing-ethics/research-publishing-ethics-guidelines-for-journals/authorship-and-contributorship: 'AI use must be declared and clearly explained ... AI does not meet the Cambridge requirements for authorship ... AI and LLM tools may not be listed as an author.' Manuscript's existing disclosure satisfies this.

EXPLICITLY ACCOMMODATED. CUP authorship policy verbatim: 'Journals may have their own policies on anonymous or pseudonymous authorship ... Where these are deployed, the standard responsibilities for authorship must be fulfilled (including legal requirements).' This is the only top-5 venue with an explicit pseudonymous-authorship clause in its policy chain. Standard responsibilities (corresponding author signs publication agreement; ORCID; accountability) are unchanged. Recommend a pre-submission email to the editor confirming the journal accepts ORCID-only identification under its CUP policy.

rejection_risk_failure_modes: Bulletin readers may view a Khinchin-specific PSLQ null result as too specialised; a clear motivation paragraph aimed at general number-theorists is required.
If editor judges the result 'not surprising enough' for the Bulletin's 'new and interesting' bar, desk rejection is fast (1 month) but bounce time is cheap.
Computational-volume papers occasionally bounce to Journal of the Australian Math Society (the longer-paper sibling); not relevant here since manuscript fits the 12-page cap.
candidate_audits.null_result_tolerance: MODERATE. The Bulletin's stated criterion is 'new and interesting' rather than 'positive'; calibration / methodology contributions are accepted when motivation is clear. The two-constant (pi and e) replication strengthens the 'new and interesting' framing.
candidate_audits.style_scope_drift: LOW. Short-note format matches the manuscript exactly; minimal rewrite needed.
candidate_audits.reviewer_pool_concern: LOW. Australian-based + general-NT pool has minimal overlap with Exp Math's PSLQ-specialist pool. Highest reviewer-pool independence among top-5.
candidate_audits.audit_verdict: UPGRADE to candidate primary or strong backup. Verified-fast decision time + verified pseudonymous policy + low reviewer-pool overlap make this the strongest non-MComp option.

revised_ranking.rationale: Literature centroid (4/5 most-cited works appeared here); double-anonymous peer review removes pseudonymity friction and isolates the manuscript from the prior Exp Math rejection shadow; AI-permissive with the manuscript's existing disclosure already satisfying AMS-COPE policy verbatim.

revised_ranking.venue: Bulletin of the Australian Mathematical Society
revised_ranking.rationale: Verified fast decision (~1 month); explicit CUP policy clause accommodating pseudonymous authorship; lowest reviewer-pool overlap with Experimental Mathematics; format and length fit exactly. Strong backup with the lowest expected wait-time-to-verdict in the candidate set.
Plausible scope (Khinchin and CF historically present), but centroid is q-series/modular rather than metric-CF/PSLQ; verification incomplete due to Springer login wall. Reserve.
Always-available archival path; bypasses journal frictions; pre-print compatible with any subsequent journal submission for green-OA-friendly venues (BAustMS and MComp both permit preprints).

candidates_dropped_at_stage3.reason: Verified HARD AI BAN (https://math.colgate.edu/~integers/submit.html) — functionally identical to NNTDM ban; not compatible with manuscript's Copilot/Claude disclosure.
Verification gaps (Springer login wall); centroid is analytic/arithmetic-geometric NT rather than metric CF/PSLQ; recommend re-audit if surfaced in future relay.
Stage-2 rank #5; scope-fit score 3; deprioritised in favor of better-fitting venues. Not a Stage-3 audit target.
Stage-2 rank #6; manuscript is evidential not algorithmic; deprioritised.
Stage-2 rank #8; OA APC barrier for pseudonymous author without institutional waiver; deprioritised.
Stage-2 rank #9; not a primary destination for CF/Khinchin material.
Stage-2 rank #11; diamond OA but lower visibility; reserved as deep fallback if all top-3 fail.
